body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

multi-turn-conversation chains three consecutive user prompts in ONE live session, asserting the parser resets state between turns (`ready → working → ready` once per turn) and that per-turn token contribution accumulates. It resolves to coverage cell `basic-turn`, exercising the same turn-end-detection primitive three times in one session rather than once. The load-bearing question is whether aider sustains a multi-turn REPL and whether the daemon flips back to `ready` between turns — not just at process exit.

**agent_supports: yes.** aider's REPL is natively multi-turn: one process holds the session open and appends every prompt + reply to the same per-cwd `.aider.chat.history.md`. `capabilities.json` records `tui: true` and `headless_mode: true`, and the committed `drive-aider-interactive.sh` drives that REPL under tmux with send/wait_turn steps — `wait_turn` blocks on each new `> Tokens:` line before the next send, so three send+wait_turn pairs are one live session. The captured regression transcript (`regression/multi-turn-conversation/transcript.md`) shows all three turns (turn-one / turn-two / turn-three) with three distinct `> Tokens:` lines on a single `aider` invocation — direct proof aider holds one session across the three turns.

**daemon_capability: full.** The aider adapter watches `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` — markdown, not JSONL. The parser opens a turn on each `#### ` user line and emits an `assistant_message` (carrying a per-call `Tokens` snapshot) at each `> Tokens:` line — but deliberately NOT a `turn_done` there, since under `--yes-always` aider may re-prompt the model within one turn. End-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by `IdleFlush` after `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` (1500ms) of transcript quiet, surfaced as `working → ready` behind the daemon's `staleWorkingRefreshInterval` (5s) `SessionDetector` poll (~5s effective latency). This is the parser's designed turn-end mechanism, not a mishandled trace: given each prompt followed by ≥~5s idle, the daemon emits all the spec-required lifecycle events — one `pid_discovered`, three `ready → working → ready` arcs on the same session_id, `TotalTokens > 0` from the per-call snapshots, and exactly one `process_exited` at teardown. `TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn` and `TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone` cover this two-stage shape. No spec-required observable is mis-handled, so the verdict is `full`; the IdleFlush latency and the cadence-fragility are captured as caveats, not a daemon `bug`.

The earlier `partial` verdict was driven by a prior recording in which the per-turn arcs blurred (a fast prompt landed before IdleFlush fired, so `TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking` behavior coalesced two turns). That is a recipe-cadence problem, not a daemon limitation: the canonical recipe now uses ≥12s inter-turn sleeps so each turn's `working → ready` settles before the next `####` arrives. The fix lives entirely in the recipe's sleep length, not in the parser — hence `daemon_capability: full` with a cadence caveat rather than `bug`/`incapable`.

**driver_capability: ready.** multi-turn-conversation needs only send + wait_turn + sleep, all implemented in `drive-aider-interactive.sh`. No gapped primitive (restart/reset_session/resume/keys/sigkill/…) is required for sequential same-session turns, so the driver is `ready`.

This refines the existing matrix row (aider/basic-turn) — the coverage cell still reads `daemon_capability: unknown`, so it should update to `full`.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider has no transcript turn-end marker; the per-turn working→ready flip is synthesized only by IdleFlush after ~1.5s quiet + the 5s detector poll (~5s effective latency). The three-turn arc is observable cleanly only when each prompt is followed by ≥~5s idle (the canonical recipe's ≥12s inter-turn sleeps).

Turn boundaries blur under fast cadence: if the next #### prompt lands before IdleFlush fires, the parser opens the new turn with no intervening turn_done and the session stays working across the boundary (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking) — a recipe-sleep concern, not a daemon defect; the 12s sleeps in the canonical recipe avoid it.

cost_tracking: false — per-turn Contribution carries token counts (sent/received) from each `> Tokens:` line but provider dollar cost is absent unless aider prints a `, $… message` suffix; the spec's TotalTokens>0 assertion holds, dollar cost may not.
Exact assistant wording and per-turn token counts vary run-to-run (model-output variance); only the structural three-turn arc, single-session count, and final ready state are stable.

sources.note: FilesUnderCWD{Filename: .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; CommandPattern /aider process match — markdown transcript, not JSONL.

Turn opens on `#### `; `> Tokens:` emits assistant_message (NOT turn_done) carrying a per-call Tokens snapshot; turn-end synthesized via IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms) — the designed mechanism, grounds daemon_capability: full.

TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn (assistant_message per call, no in-band turn_done); TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone (idle synthesizes turn_done); TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking (fast next prompt → no per-turn reset — the cadence caveat).
staleWorkingRefreshInterval = 5 * time.Second — the working-session poll cadence that sets the ~5s effective working→ready latency for idle-flush turn-end (caveat, not a daemon bug).

tmux REPL driver implements send + wait_turn + sleep; wait_turn blocks on a new `> Tokens:` line; three send/wait_turn cycles on one live session — no gapped primitive needed, grounds driver_capability: ready.
Captured real run: one aider invocation, three turns (turn-one/turn-two/turn-three) with three `> Tokens:` lines — proves aider holds one session across the three turns (agent_supports: yes).

Captured daemon events from a recording whose inter-turn sleeps were too short (7s): a single ready→working and coalesced arcs before terminal process_exited — evidence the blur is a recipe-cadence problem (fixed by ≥12s sleeps), not a parser limitation.
headless_mode: true (the Primitive-exercised anchor key), tui: true, streaming_output: true; cost_tracking: false, structured_output_jsonl: false (markdown transcript, no per-turn dollar cost).
